title: Spot the content-type XSS in a download endpoint
prompt:
Identify and fix the XSS vulnerability in this download action:

```elixir
def download(conn, %{"content_type" => ct, "body" => body}) do
  conn
  |> put_resp_content_type(ct)
  |> send_resp(200, body)
end
```

An attacker can set `content_type=text/html` and upload `<script>alert(1)</script>` as the body, producing stored XSS on any subsequent request that fetches the file. Rewrite the action so (a) the content-type is forced to `application/octet-stream` or `text/plain` regardless of user input, (b) a `content-disposition: attachment; filename=...` header is added so browsers download rather than render, and (c) the `body` is still returned as the response body. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/download_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires recognizing that a user-supplied content-type is a stored XSS vector, not just a raw/1 issue.
research_citation: EEF Security WG: 'A malicious user could set the content_type to text/html, and upload an HTML document that executes JavaScript.'
